observations.recommendation:
1.12. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
c) Require the Inventory Committee to submit an explanation to the Audit
Team why no amount was reflected in the Report on Physical Count of
PPE (RPCPPE) on the following properties:
 Road Network
 Flood Control System
 Water Supply System
 Power Supply System
 Seaport System
 School Buildings
 Hospitals and Health Centers
 Markets
 Slaughterhouses
 Other Structures
 Office Equipment
 Communication Equipment
 Sports Equipment
 Motor Vehicles
 Watercrafts
 Other Transportation Equipment
 Furniture & Fixtures
b.) Require the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to submit
an explanation to the Audit Team why Property Ledger Cards and
Property Cards, respectively, were not maintained.

1.13. We also recommended for the Municipal Accountant to:
a.) reclassify the items which are below the P15,000.00 threshold to the
Inventory account, accomplish the ICS for its issuance to end-users;
b.) recognize in the books all PPE items found during the physical count which
were not yet recorded.

1.14. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Property
Officer reconcile their records pursuant to the procedures outlined per COA
Circular No. 2020-006 (One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balance) to arrive
at a reliable PPE balance as of yearend; and

1.15. Finally, we recommended that henceforth, the Inventory Committee conduct
a complete physical count of all properties of the LGU every year and prepare
a completely filled out RPCPPE.

2.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the donated land and
sea ambulance in the books and compute the depreciation expense thereof
from the period these were received.

3.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a.) submit justification why the Generator Set was paid despite unperformed items
of work by the supplier on installation and commissioning;
b.) require the supplier for the immediate installation of the generator set to
maximize the utilization of the equipment.

3.9 These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of
Suspension, if warranted.

4.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant:

a. Submit explanation why AIR as well as the PAR were not attached to
the DV;
b. Henceforth, ascertain completeness of the documentary requirements
for the payment of deliveries of equipment before signing in the
certification box to this effect and the Inspectorate Team to prepare the
AIR on all items received by the LGU and furnish the Audit Team a
copy within 24 hours from acceptance.

4.8 We also recommended that the Property Officer issue PAR to the end-user
upon issuance of equipment/property and have this renewed once in every
three (3) years as required by existing regulations.

5.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

c. require the Municipal Accountant to submit an explanation why payment
for fuel, oil and lubricants were not supported with complete documents;
d. direct the Supply Officer concerned to comply with the reporting and
documentary requirements for monitoring the reasonableness of the fuel
issued to the drivers of the LGU vehicles;

5.13 We also recommended that henceforth, management shall procure fuel in accordance
with RA 9184 and its IRR. Also, the corresponding utilization shall be recognized
in the books based on duly accomplished Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption.

5.13 This is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted.

6.12 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

a.) submit explanation to the Audit Team why the Municipal Treasurer was
granted additional cash advances even if previous cash advances were not
yet liquidated;
b.) submit explanation why liquidation vouchers were approved despite absence
of complete supporting documents.
c.) Henceforth, see to it that previous cash advances are liquidated first before
new cash advances are granted and complete supporting documents should
be attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before forwarding to the Local
Chief Executive for approval of payment.

6.13 We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit explanation why
RERs were accepted as proof of payment for transactions with amount
exceeding P1,000.00.

6.14 Lastly, we recommended that persons responsible submit to the Audit Team
the lacking supporting documents presented in Table 8.

7.9 We recommended that justifications be submitted by the following:
a) The BAC committee on why procurement of infrastructure projects was
done “by administration” despite the fact that the LGU does not own the
equipment necessary to execute the project as provided in RA 9184;
b) The Municipal Engineer on why PPMP on infrastructure projects were
not prepared and included in the APP;
c) Municipal Treasurer for not demanding the issuance of an Official Receipt
as proof of payment for the use of the construction equipment.
d) This is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Disallowance, if
warranted.

7.10 Moving forward, we recommended that the:
a) BAC Committee undertake projects by administration only if the LGU has
the tools and equipment needed to execute the project.
b) Municipal Engineer prepare and submit PPMP to be included in the APP
of the agency; and
c) Municipal Accountant to approve disbursements only if supported with
appropriate documents.

8.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the lacking
necessary supporting documents as indicated above and the Municipal
Treasurer to justify the payment out of the PCF of expenses amounting to
P15,000 and above.

8.7 This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension, if warranted.

9.12 We recommended that the Local DRRM Officer should submit a justification,
for the Audit Team’s review and evaluation, why payment for purchase of
Printer, Repair of Air Conditioner, purchase of various supplies and
materials, meals and snacks and Construction of Tombs at Naval Public
Cemetery and other charges not in relation to LDRRM programs and projects
were charged to LDRRMF.

9.13 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit
justification why payments to these transactions should not be disallowed in
audit despite the absence authority, ineligible expenditures and lack of the
required supporting documents.

9.14 Finally, we recommended that, henceforth, a thorough review on the validity,
propriety of transactions and the completeness of supporting documents shall
be made by LGU officials concerned.

10.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the
subject Disbursement Vouchers to the Audit Team. A Notice of Suspension or
Disallowance will be issued on these transactions.

10.8 Henceforth, ensure that the submission of financial reports and its
attachments be made within the prescribed period.

1. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare an adjusting entry, restoring the P1,562,870.00 to Due to NGA Account and recording the deduction from Trust Liabilities- DRRMF by the same amount, to present the affected accounts in order.
2. Moving forward, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant should record the payment of monthly allowance to national government personnel assigned in LGU-Naval to Professional Services account.

3. We reiterated our prior year recommendation that the Municipal Accountant should disclose the necessary information on the Road Network account in the Notes to the Financial Statements every year in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

4. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Finance Office coordinate with the Barangay Officials and implement identification of eligible beneficiaries and obtain reliable data for inclusion in the municipality’s database so that a list of eligible beneficiaries could easily be obtained.

5. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the BAC Chairperson submit justification why procurement was not through Emergency Procurement in accordance with GPPB Circular No. 1-2020 and why payment was through cash advance of the Municipal Government Treasurer and not by check. 6. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit justification why payment was approved as liquidation despite the absence of appropriate supporting documents.

7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submits an explanation why procurement of meals and snacks was on reimbursement basis. 8. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit to the Audit Team for evaluation the Delivery Receipt, Acceptance and Inspection Report and Attendance Sheet; and 9. Justification why reimbursements were made despite absence of supporting documents. 10. Further, we recommended that the BAC Chairperson should include procurement of meals and snack in the Annual Procurement Plan of the LGU.

11. We recommended that the Local DRRM Officer should submit a justification, for the Audit Team’s review and evaluation, why payment for payment for tables, chairs and television should be charged to LDRRMF. 12. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit justification why payments to these transactions should not be disallowed in audit despite the absence authority, ineligible expenditures and lack of the required supporting documents. 13. Finally, we recommended that, henceforth, a thorough review on the validity, propriety of transactions and the completeness of supporting documents shall be made by LGU officials concerned.

13. We recommended that the Municipal LDRRM Officer should prepare a revised LDRRMFIP to incorporate the purchase of roofing materials funded from prior year’s unexpended balance. 14. We also recommended that the Municipal Development Officer should prepare and submit a revised AIP to incorporate all COVID-19 PPA’s including the PPAs funded from Local DRRM Fund.

15. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Municipal Treasurer and the Human Resource Management Officer, should stop the practice of Job Order personnel to perform collection function.

16. We recommended that the management submit legal basis for the grant of honorarium to the Jail Warden/BGMP, otherwise, they should stop the payment of the same to the subject personnel. 17. We also recommended that the amount be refunded by the recipient, without prejudice to the joint and solidary liability of those who authorized and certified the legality and propriety of the payment. 18. We further recommended that henceforth, the concerned municipal officials should only approve transactions which are duly authorized by law to avoid disallowance in audit.

19. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents, otherwise a Notice of Suspension will be issued. Henceforth, ensure that the submission of financial reports and its attachments be made within the prescribed period. 20. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant submit explanation for the Audit Team’s evaluation why these Disbursement Vouchers were not submitted within the reglementary period.

21. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Assessor, review immediately the Inventory Report for Roads and Condition Survey and compare its contents with the records of the Municipal Accountant in order to reconcile the difference between the balances in their records and report. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the road lot component of road projects amounting to P3,342,039.48 and the subsequent road projects of LGU-Naval; and maintain property ledger cards for each road components to support the recorded amount of the Road Network account and to arrive at an accurate balance of the account.

22. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the Accumulated Depreciation computed per Road Network Ledger Cards against the amount recorded per GL audit and prepare adjusting entries, where necessary.

25. We recommended that the Municipal Assessor: a) coordinate with the Provincial Environment and Natural Resources Officer (PENRO) Biliran for assistance on the steps to be taken for the titling of lands owned by LGU-Naval; b) furnish the Municipal Treasurer, as custodian of TCTs, and the Municipal Accountant with copies of titled real properties of the LGU so as to reflect in the books the accurate information on said properties.

26. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the recipient-LGUs to submit Utilization Reports and desist from granting additional funds to the same barangay unless the previous release had been fully accounted for. 27. File a request with COA for authority to write-off dormant receivables which are no longer identifiable pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and 28. Evaluate the long outstanding Due from LGU balances, determined if liquidation is still possible and consider the writing–off of dormant unliquidated fund transfers.

29. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create the Disposal Committee to initiate the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties pursuant to PD 1445 and require the said Committee to recommend the mode of its disposal. Also, to require the Property Officer to follow the disposal procedures prescribed in RA No. 7160. 30. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to derecognize from the books the properties after its proper disposal.

31. It is recommended that the BAC and the Accounting Section should strictly follow the stated rules and regulations on government procurement process pursuant to RA 9184 and specifically the following courses of action; 32. To diligently furnish the Auditor the Approved Contracts together with the required supporting documents within five (5) days upon the perfection of the contract to preclude suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions during post audit. 33. To facilitate the submission within the prescribed period, it is suggested that the documents be placed in an expanded envelope for each project with a label indicating the Name of the Project, Project ID, Location, Contractor, Contract Amount, Source of Fund and Date of Bidding. 34. Please refer to Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for the documentary requirements for the payments related to infrastructure projects particularly the advance/progress/final payments, release of retention money and other related transactions.
35. We recommended that the Local Chief Officers and Employees require the accounting office to request for the writing off of the questioned dormant accounts in manners prescribed by COA rules.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry, restoring the P1,562,870.00 to Due to NGA Account and recording the deduction from Trust Liabilities- DRRMF by the same amount, to present the affected accounts in order.
Moving forward, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant should record the payment of monthly allowance to national government personnel assigned in LGU-Naval to Professional Services account.

We reiterated our prior year recommendation that the Municipal Accountant should disclose the necessary information on the Road Network account in the Notes to the Financial Statements every year in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the MSWDO, in coordination with the Barangay Officials should conduct identification of eligible beneficiaries and obtain reliable data for inclusion in the municipality’s database so that a list of eligible beneficiaries could easily be obtained.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the BAC Chairperson submit justification why procurement was not through Emergency Procurement and why payment was through cash advance; and that the Municipal Accountant should submit justification why payment was approved as liquidation despite absence of supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit explanation why procurement of meals and snacks was on reimbursement basis and submit Delivery Receipt, AIR and Attendance Sheet; and justification why reimbursements were made despite absence of supporting documents. Also recommended BAC include procurement of meals and snacks in APP.

We recommended that the Local DRRM Officer should submit justification why payments were charged to LDRRMF; Municipal Accountant to submit justification why payments should not be disallowed despite absence of authority and supporting documents; and that thorough review on validity and completeness of supporting documents be made henceforth.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer prepare a revised LDRRMFIP to incorporate the purchases funded from prior year’s unexpended balance and that the Municipal Development Officer prepare a revised AIP to incorporate all COVID-19 PPAs funded from Local DRRMF.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer and HR Officer stop the practice of Job Order personnel performing collection functions.

We recommended management submit legal basis for the grant of honorarium otherwise stop payment, recommend refund by recipient and that concerned officials approve only transactions duly authorized by law.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking DVs and supporting documents or a Notice of Suspension will be issued; ensure timely submission of financial reports and attachments; and Municipal Treasurer and Accountant submit explanation for non-submission.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Engineer and Assessor review the Inventory Report for Roads and Condition Survey and reconcile differences; Municipal Accountant to record road lot component of road projects and maintain property ledger cards for road components.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant reconcile Accumulated Depreciation per Road Network Ledger Cards against GL and prepare necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended the Municipal Assessor coordinate with PENRO for titling assistance; furnish Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant with copies of titled real properties to reflect accurate book information.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive require recipient-LGUs to submit Utilization Reports and desist from granting additional funds until previous releases were accounted for; request COA authority to write-off dormant receivables; evaluate long outstanding Due from LGU balances for possible write-off.

We recommended creation of Disposal Committee to initiate disposal as per PD 1445, require Property Officer to follow disposal procedures in RA 7160 and Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries after disposal.

We recommended BAC and Accounting Section strictly follow procurement rules and furnish Approved Contracts with supporting documents within five (5) days upon perfection; suggest placing documents in labeled expanded envelopes; refer to COA Circular 2012-001 for documentary requirements.
We recommended the accounting office request writing off of the questioned dormant accounts as prescribed by COA rules.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Assessor, review immediately the Inventory Report for Roads and Bridges and Condition Survey and compare its contents with the records of the Municipal Accountant in order to reconcile the difference between the balances in their records and report.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Record the road lot component of road projects amounting to P3,342,039.48.00 and the subsequent road projects of LGU- Naval; 

List all barangay road projects implemented by LGU-Naval and derecognize their costs in the books of the Municipality, and record the same in the books of the concerned barangay.

Maintain property ledger cards for each road components to support the recorded amount of the Road Network account and to arrive at an accurate balance of the account.
observations.managementAction:
Finishing Inventory of Road networks

Requested the Mayor to create an Inventory Committee














Started the preparation of property ledger cards to include the various road networks component

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant include the necessary disclosures on the Road Network account in the Notes to the Financial Statements every year.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor: 

coordinate with the Provincial Environment and National Resources Officer (PENRO) Biliran for assistance on the steps to be taken for the titling of lands owned by LGU-Naval through the Land Administration Management
 Project (LAMP); and,

furnish the Municipal Treasurer, as custodian of TCTs, and the Municipal Accountant with copies of titled real properties of the LGU so as to reflect in the books the accurate information on said properties.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 

a)Furnish the Assessor with the list of parcels of land recorded in the books, tally their records and record only the lots with TCT; and 
Re-classify the cost of lots on road right of way to road lot component in the Road Network account.
observations.managementAction: Only three titles of land were completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to submit a final evaluation and status report on the two BuB projects on Community Fish Landing Center project and the YRRP as to its viability and continuity.  If the projects cannot be implemented during CY 2020, return the funds to the National Government.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Disbursement Voucher for the return of all unutilized balances to the Source Agencies in order to clear the Municipality of its liabilities to the NGAs.
observations.managementAction:
The project is not implementable because prices per program of work is not enough due to high prices.

The project will be implemented by administration as per instruction subject to LGU counterpart to complete the project.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor: 
(a) revise the Memorandum of Agreement with the barangays to include provisions on the time frame within which the grantee is to implement the purpose of the funds including the period of submission of the Fund Utilization Report; and (b) desist from granting additional funds to the same barangay unless the previous release had been fully accounted for.

We also reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to recipient-LGUs who’s Utilization Report is already due and require their compliance.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare a quarterly schedule on the composition of the Due from LGU account to identify which LGU has not submitted its Utilization Report on the date prescribed in the MOA;

Provide the Municipal Mayor with the list of LGUs which failed to submit Utilization Reports despite the demand as basis in the deferment of additional fund transfer;

File a request with COA for authority to write off dormant receivables which are no longer identifiable pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and

Evaluate the long outstanding Due from LGU balances, determined if liquidation is still possible and consider the writing–off   of dormant unliquidated fund transfers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create the Disposal Committee to initiate the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties and require the said Committee to recommend the mode of disposal.  Also, require the Property Officer to follow the disposal procedures prescribed in RA No. 7160.

Further, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to derecognize from the books the properties after its proper disposal.
observations.managementAction:
Disposal Committee was already created.








An adjusting entries shall be made upon disposal of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that the BAC and the Accounting Section should strictly follow the stated rules and regulations on government procurement process pursuant to RA 9184 and specifically comply the hereunder courses of action; to wit:

To diligently furnish the Auditor the Approved Contracts together with the required supporting documents within five (5) days upon the perfection of the contract to preclude suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions during post audit.

To facilitate the submission within the prescribed period, it is suggested that the documents be placed in an expanded envelope for each project with a label indicating the Name of the Project, Project ID, Location, Contractor, Contract Amount, Source of Fund and Date of Bidding.

Please refer to Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for the documentary requirements for the payments related to infrastructure projects particularly the advance/progress/final payments, release of retention money and other related transactions.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the accounting office to request for the writing off of the questioned dormant accounts in manners prescribed by COA rules.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had a difficulty to collate the required documents for their request to write-off the dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare an adjusting entry, restoring the ₱1,562,870.00 to Due to NGA Account and recording the deduction from Trust Liabilities-DRRMF by the same amount, to present the affected accounts in order.
Moving forward, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant should record the payment of monthly allowance to national government personnel assigned in LGU-Naval to Professional Services account.

We reiterated our prior year recommendation that the Municipal Accountant should disclose  the necessary information on the Road Network account in the Notes to the Financial Statements every year in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that the MSWDO, in coordination with the Barangay Officials should conduct identification of all low-income families of each barangay of the municipality and obtain reliable data for inclusion in the municipality’s database so that a list of eligible beneficiaries could easily be obtained.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the BAC Chairperson submit justification why procurement was not through Emergency Procurement in accordance with GPPB Circular No. 1-2020 and why payment was made through cash advance of the Municipal Treasurer and not by check.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit justification why disbursements were approved as liquidation despite the absence of the appropriate supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submits an explanation why procurement of meals and snacks was on reimbursement basis.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should:
a.	submit to the Audit Team for evaluation the Delivery Receipt, Acceptance and Inspection Report and Attendance Sheet; and
b.	Justification why reimbursements were made despite absence of supporting documents.
Further, we recommended that the BAC Chairperson should include procurement of meals and snack in the Annual Procurement Plan of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local DRRM Officer should submit a justification, for the Audit Team’s review and evaluation, why payment for tables, chairs and television should be charged to LDRRMF.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit justification why these vouchers were indorsed for payment despite the deficiencies of the supporting documents.  Henceforth, a thorough review on the completeness of supporting documents shall be made.

We recommended that the Municipal LDRRM Officer should prepare a revised LDRRMFIP to incorporate the purchase of roofing materials funded from prior year’s unexpended Local DRRM Fund.
We also recommended that the Municipal Planning Development Officer should prepare a revised AIP to incorporate all COVID-19 PPA’s including the PPAs funded from Local DRRM Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Municipal Treasurer and the Human Resource Management Officer, should stop the practice of allowing Job Order personnel to perform collection function.

We recommended that management submit legal basis for the grant of honorarium to the Jail Warden/BGMP, otherwise, they should stop the payment of the same to the subject personnel.
We also recommended that the amount be refunded by the recipient, without prejudice to the joint and solidary liability of those who authorized and certified the legality and propriety of the payment.
We further recommended that henceforth, the concerned municipal officials should only approve transactions which are duly authorized by law to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents, otherwise a Notice of Suspension will be issued. Henceforth, ensure that the submission of financial reports and its attachments be made within the prescribe period.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant submit explanation for the Audit Team’s evaluation why these Disbursement Vouchers were not submitted within the reglementary period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 
request from the Municipal Engineer for a copy of the Local Road Network Inventory Report with an updated assessed values of each component of the municipal roads, including the estimated useful life of each component;

compute the correct depreciation on the assets under Road Network account, based on the current assessed values, using straight line method but without residual value of the depreciable components pursuant to Item IV.13-14 of COA Circular No. 2015-08; and,

reconcile the Accumulated Depreciation computed per Road Network Ledger Cards against the amount recorded per GL and prepare adjusting entries, where necessary.
observations.managementAction: Lack of staff to do the reconciliation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO immediately review their respective records and confer with each other to reconcile the difference between the balance shown in the records of the Office of the Municipal Accountant and the balance reflected in the Inventory List of Items for DRRM of the MDRRMO.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant supervise closely the works of the accounting personnel and review all accounting records and reports that they are handling in order that its balances are the ones reflected in the FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local Development Council:

Speed up the implementation of projects which were funded and identified in CY 2019, including all current projects in the Annual Investment Plan;

Include only in the AIP those projects which are necessary, viable and implementable during the year.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a record book to monitor the cash equivalent to the 20% Development Fund, including its Continuing Appropriations, to ensure that the cash of unimplemented projects are not utilized for other purposes and remain intact in the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) identify the specific public infrastructures acquired in CY 2014 and prior years; (2) record the same in the appropriate Infrastructure Asset accounts; (3) maintain property ledger cards for each public infrastructure, and; (4) prepare a lapsing schedule to compute depreciation of each asset.

We recommended that the Department Heads concerned implement the programs and projects funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA.  Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer return immediately all unutilized funds of completed programs and projects.

We recommended that the Market Board, through the Municipal Treasurer:

Revise the Contract of Lease to coincide with the provisions in the Revised Revenue Code of 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Revoke the contract of lease with the current stall holders once the authorization from the Sanguniang Bayan is received to include vital provisions that will protect the interest of the Municipality.


To recommend to the Sanguniang Bayan to amend SB Resolution No. 453 series of 2016 or the Naval Gym Lease Ordinance of 2016 to include stipulation that shall expressly include the period of lease, the imposition of surcharges, penalties and/or interest on the unpaid rentals as well as the prohibition on sub-leasing, and;

Draft new contract tailored on the amended ordinance.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer discontinue the payment of regular expenses from her cash advances intended for specific purpose.  Also, obtain cash advance for financial assistance at an amount equivalent to the number of identified recipients only and pay petty expenses from the petty cash fund.

We recommend for management to strictly enforce the provisions on the grant, utilization and control of cash advances.  We reiterate the following courses of actions:
Stop the practice of granting another cash advances to those officials and employees with outstanding cash advances.

Stop the practice of granting special cash advances for payment of regular procurement.  All purchases shall be paid thru checks except those paid under the petty cash fund.

Management should take alternative courses of actions towards the maximum utilization of the twelve (12) buses and the eradication or if possible the minimization of maintenance expenses, the latter while preserving said assets.
observations.managementAction: These properties are unserviceable and beyond economic repair.

observations.recommendation: 33. We recommend that the Accountant conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the receivables, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers and determine the concerned debtors, accountable officers (Regular and Special Disbursing Officers, Collecting Officers, Cashiers) and the source and implementing government entities concerned.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive together with the Accounting office were requested to collate the required documents for their request to write-off the dormant accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant had difficulty to collate the required documents for their request to write-off the dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: 34. Management should take alternative courses of actions towards the maximum utilization of the twelve (12) buses and the eradication or if possible the minimization of the maintenance expenses, the Municipality of Maripipi, Biliran and there were no funds available for its maintenance.
observations.managementAction: The buses were currently at dumpsite. The Municipality of Maripipi, Biliran to determine the availability of funds for maintenance.
observations.reason: The twelve (12) buses were not maintained and were currently at dumpsite.

observations.recommendation:
1.10. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, with the assistance of the
Municipal Engineer and Municipal Assessor, review immediately the
Inventory Report for Roads and Bridges and Condition Survey and compare its
contents with the records of the Municipal Accountant in order to reconcile
the difference between the balances in their records and report.
1.11. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Record the road lot component of road projects amounting to
P3,342,039.48.00 and the subsequent road projects of LGU- Naval;
b) List all barangay road projects implemented by LGU-Naval and
derecognize their costs in the books of the Municipality, and record the
same in the books of the concerned barangay.
c) Maintain property ledger cards for each road components to support the
recorded amount of the Road Network account and to arrive at an
accurate balance of the account.

2.13. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) request from the Municipal Engineer for a copy of the Local Road
Network Inventory Report with an updated assessed values of each
component of the municipal roads, including the estimated useful life of
each component;
b) compute the correct depreciation on the assets under Road Network
account, based on the current assessed values, using straight line method
but without residual value of the depreciable components pursuant to
Item IV.13-14 of COA Circular No. 2015-08; and,
c) reconcile the Accumulated Depreciation computed per Road Network
Ledger Cards against the amount recorded per GL and prepare
adjusting entries, where necessary.

3.5. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant include the necessary
disclosures on the Road Network account in the Notes to the Financial
Statements every year.

4.13. We recommended that the Municipal Assessor:
a) coordinate with the Provincial Environment and National Resources
Officer (PENRO) Biliran for assistance on the steps to be taken for the
titling of lands owned by LGU-Naval through the Land Administration
Management Project (LAMP); and,
b) furnish the Municipal Treasurer, as custodian of TCTs, and the
Municipal Accountant with copies of titled real properties of the LGU so
as to reflect in the books the accurate information on said properties.
4.14. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Furnish the Assessor with the list of parcels of land recorded in the
books, tally their records and record only the lots with TCT; and
b) Re-classify the cost of lots on road right of way to road lot component in
the Road Network account.

5.6. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO
immediately review their respective records and confer with each other to
reconcile the difference between the balance shown in the records of the
Office of the Municipal Accountant and the balance reflected in the
Inventory List of Items for DRRM of the MDRRMO.

6.7. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant supervise closely the works
of the accounting personnel and review all accounting records and reports
that they are handling in order that its balances are the ones reflected in the
FS.

7.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Local
Development Council:

a) Speed up the implementation of projects which were funded and
identified in CY 2019, including all current projects in the Annual
Investment Plan;
b) Include only in the AIP those projects which are necessary, viable and
implementable during the year.
c) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a record book to monitor the
cash equivalent to the 20% Development Fund, including its Continuing
Appropriations, to ensure that the cash of unimplemented projects are
not utilized for other purposes and remain intact in the General Fund.

8.12. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Agriculturist to submit a final evaluation and status report on the two BuB
projects on Community Fish Landing Center project and the YRRP as to its
viability and continuity. If the projects cannot be implemented during CY
2020, return the funds to the National Government.
8.13. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the
Disbursement Voucher for the return of all unutilized balances to the Source
Agencies in order to clear the Municipality of its liabilities to the NGAs.

9.9. We reiterated our prior’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor: (a)
revise the Memorandum of Agreement with the barangays to include
provisions on the time frame within which the grantee is to implement the
purpose of the funds including the period of submission of the Fund
Utilization Report; and (b) desist from granting additional funds to the same
barangay unless the previous release had been fully accounted for.

9.10. We also reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal
Accountant to send demand letters to recipient-LGUs who’s Utilization
Report is already due and require their compliance.

9.11. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Prepare a quarterly schedule on the composition of the Due from LGU
account to identify which LGU has not submitted its Utilization Report
on the date prescribed in the MOA;
b) Provide the Municipal Mayor with the list of LGUs which failed to
submit Utilization Reports despite the demand as basis in the deferment
of additional fund transfer;
c) File a request with COA for authority to write off dormant receivables
which are no longer identifiable pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005
dated December 19, 2016; and
d) Evaluate the long outstanding Due from LGU balances, determined if
liquidation is still possible and consider the writingoff of dormant
unliquidated fund transfers.

10.15. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor comply with the quarterly
remittance of RPT/SET shares of barangays and to the Province.
10.16. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) Review RPT/SET collections of prior years up to CY 2019 to determine
the shares of each Barangay of LGU-Naval and the Provincial share;
b) Prepare immediately the Disbursement Vouchers for the release of prior
years’ payables on RPT/SET shares to the recipient barangays and to the
Province;
c) Prepare a monthly schedule showing the amount of the RPT/SET
payable to the Province and each barangay and record in individual
subsidiary ledger; and
d) Prepare, within five (5) days after the end of each quarter, the
Disbursement Vouchers for the release of the RPT/SET shares to the
recipient barangays and to the Province, without need for demand.

1. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (1) identify the specific public infrastructure accounts; (2) record the same in the appropriate Property accounts; (3) maintain property ledger cards for each public infrastructure, and; (4) prepare a lapsing schedule to compute depreciation of each asset.
observations.managementAction: Started the identification of the specific public infrastructure accounts.
observations.reason: There are still many revisions and adjustments on the accounts.

observations.recommendation: 2. We recommended that the Department Heads concerned implement the programs and projects funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA. Also, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer return immediately all unutilized funds of completed programs and projects.
observations.managementAction: Delay was due to no bidder for two phileps postings.

observations.recommendation: 3. Issue demand letters to Barangay Chairpersons who failed to submit Utilization Reports on funds received from the Municipality; 4. Desist from granting additional funds to the same barangay unless the previous release had been fully accounted for; 5. Revise the MOA with the barangays to include provisions on the time frame within which the grantee is to implement the purpose of the funds including the period of submission of the Fund Utilization Report.
observations.managementAction: Issued demand letters. Hold the releases to barangays.

observations.recommendation: 6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (1) direct the Municipal Engineer to execute all projects planned for implementation during the year and to submit a report to the Municipal Planning Officer on a quarterly basis, and; (2) require the Municipal Planning Officer to monitor and evaluate the implementation of projects in the AIP and submit a report thereof to the Local Development Council, through the Office of the Local Chief Executive.
observations.managementAction: Realigned the projects which were implemented during the year and by the Department Heads the excess funds were returned.
observations.reason: There were projects still not implemented.

observations.recommendation: 7. Issue demand letters to all delinquent stallholders requiring the prompt payment of past due rentals and impose the pertinent surcharges thereon; 8. Enforce the automatic cancellation of the lease contract for stallholders that have been delinquent for three (3) consecutive months; 9. Impose closure of stalls of delinquent lessees with cancelled lease contracts; 10. Prepare a Monthly Report of Delinquent Stallholders to monitor the stall occupants who are not diligently paying their monthly rentals, and; 11. Revise the Contract of Lease to coincide with the provisions in the Revised Revenue Code of 2016.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were issued.
observations.reason: Revision is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: 12. Revoke the contract of lease with the current stall holders once the related ordinance received to include vital provisions that will protect the interest of the Municipality. 13. To recommend to the Sanguniang Bayan to amend SB Resolution No. 453 series of 2016 or the Naval Gym Lease Ordinance of 2016 to include stipulation that shall expressly include the period of lease, the imposition of surcharges, penalties and/or interest on the unpaid rentals as well as the prohibition on sub-leasing, and; 14. Draft new contract tailored on the amended ordinance.
observations.managementAction: The contract of lease was amended with the current stall holders once the authorization was received.
observations.reason: Revision is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: 15. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant with a certified List of Taxpayers and the Amount Due and Collectible at the start of every year as basis in recording a amount of Receivables from Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer prepared and furnished the Municipal Accountant with a certified List of Taxpayers and Amount Due and Collectible at the start of every year.

observations.recommendation: 16. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a memorandum directing the officials and employees concerned to submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption supported with gasoline Issue Slips and duly filled-up trip tickets to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording and to the Audit Team for review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Officials and employees concerned were directed to submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption together with gasoline slips and trip tickets.

observations.recommendation: 17. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create the Committee to initiate the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties and require the said Committee to recommend the mode of disposal. Also, require the Property Officer to follow the disposal procedures prescribed in RA No. 7160. 18. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to derecognize from the books the properties upon disposal.
observations.managementAction: Disposal Committee was already created and initiated the disposal. An adjusting entry shall be made after its proper disposal of unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: Waiting for the decision of the Committee.

observations.recommendation: 19. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate the MSWDO to formulate, for his approval, written policies or guidelines to establish the qualification of beneficiaries, the kind and amount of aid to be given, including control measures to avoid duplication, in order to preclude suspicion of favoritism, bias and irregularities in the process. 20. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor minimize disbursements on financial assistance, and limit the same based on urgent and absolute necessity only, to ensure that the funds of the Municipality will not be easily depleted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor designated the MSWDO to formulate the written policies and guidelines and implement control measures.

observations.recommendation: 21. We recommend that management strictly enforce the provisions on the grant, utilization and control of cash advances. We reiterate the courses of action: 30. Require the officials and employees concerned to immediately liquidate their cash advances for official travels within the prescribed period after the return to their permanent official station and as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served. 31. Stop the practice of granting special cash advances to officials and employees even if previous cash advances were not yet settled; 32. Stop the practice of granting cash advances for payment of regular procurement. All purchases shall be paid thru checks except those paid under the petty cash fund.
observations.managementAction: Some minor adjustment on financial and medical expenses were discontinued and cash advances intended for specific purpose were used; cash advances were regulated.

observations.recommendation: 22. The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant a description and cost segregation of road projects. 23. The Inventory Report on the Physical Count of the local road networks and the preparation of the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network was not prepared. 24. The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant maintain the required Local Road Network Property Ledger Cards, respectively.
observations.managementAction: A description of the infrastructure projects was forwarded by the Municipal Engineer. The Local Road Network Inventory Report was to be prepared by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: They have difficulty to determine the cost of the infrastructure projects from prior year because some projects were already relocked.

observations.recommendation: 25. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to record the accumulated depreciation of each public infrastructure account as of December 31, 2016 amounting to P2,645,498.48. Also, compute the monthly accumulated depreciation of each public infrastructure in order to present the PPE at its depreciated replacement cost and record the appropriate expense account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the required adjusting entries and computed the accumulated depreciation.

observations.recommendation: 26. We recommend that the concerned Department Heads accelerate the implementation of the programs and projects funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA. Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer should submit immediately the Liquidation Reports and other supporting documents to the Source Agencies, including the return of unutilized fund of completed programs and projects.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2019, Department Heads implemented the projects during the year and the excess funds were returned.
observations.reason: There were difficulties encountered in the implementation of some projects.

observations.recommendation: 27. To diligently furnish the Auditor the Approved Contracts together with the required supporting documents within five (5) days upon the perfection of the contract to preclude suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions during post audit. 28. To facilitate the submission within the prescribed period, it is suggested that the documents be placed in an expanded envelope for each project with a label indicating the Name of the Project, Project ID, Location, Contractor, Contract Amount, Source of Fund and Date of Bidding. 29. Please refer to Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for the documentary requirements for the payments related to infrastructure projects particularly the advance/progress/final payments, release of retention money and other related transactions.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat submit the approved contracts and supporting documents within the period required by the regulations on government procurement.

observations.recommendation: 30. Require the officials and employees concerned to immediately liquidate their cash advances for official travels within the prescribed period after the return to their permanent official station and as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served; 31. Stop the practice of officials and employees being granted another cash advances while they still have outstanding cash advances; 32. Stop the practice of payments out of cash advances and adopt check issuances as the regular mode of disbursement except those paid under petty cash fund.
observations.managementAction: Officials and employees concerned were required to liquidate cash advances. Some practices were stopped and adjustments made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create the Disposal Committee to initiate the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties and require the said Committee to recommend the mode of disposal.  Also, require the Property Officer to follow the disposal procedures prescribed in RA No. 7160.

Further, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to derecognize from the books the properties after its proper disposal.
observations.reason:
They promised to create a Disposal Committee to initiate the disposal of unserviceable properties.





Not Implemented

An adjusting entries shall be made upon disposal of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer discontinue the payment of regular expenses from her cash advances intended for specific purpose.  Also, obtain cash advance for financial assistance at an amount equivalent to the number of identified recipients only and pay petty expenses from the petty cash fund.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer explained that she was the only Accountable Officer bonded with huge amount to have a special cash advance.  Most of the special cash advances in CY 2018 were for Typhoon Urduja.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that the BAC and the Accounting Section should strictly follow the stated rules and regulations on government procurement process pursuant to RA 9184 and specifically comply the hereunder courses of action; to wit:

To diligently furnish the Auditor the Approved Contracts together with the required supporting documents within five (5) days upon the perfection of the contract to preclude suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions during post audit.

To facilitate the submission within the prescribed period, it is suggested that the documents be placed in an expanded envelope for each project with a label indicating the Name of the Project, Project ID, Location, Contractor, Contract Amount, Source of Fund and Date of Bidding.

Please refer to Section 9.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for the documentary requirements for the payments related to infrastructure projects particularly the advance/progress/final payments, release of retention money and other related transactions.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the accounting office to request for the writing off of the questioned dormant accounts in manners prescribed by COA rules.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had a difficulty to collate the required documents for their request to write-off the dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: Management should take alternative courses of actions towards the maximum utilization of the twelve (12) buses and the eradication or if possible the minimization of maintenance expenses, the latter while preserving said assets.
observations.reason:
The Twelve (12) buses were not officially turned over to the municipality of Naval by the Municipality of Maripipi, Biliran and there were no funds available for its maintenance.

The buses were currently at dumpsite.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to remove from the books of the LGU the Infrastructure Assets totaling P4,491,064.00 transferred to the barangays based on the Certificate of Turn-Over and Acceptance signed by the recipients Punong Barangays.   Also, ensure that the said properties are recorded by the Barangay Bookkeepers in the books of the recipient barangays.
observations.managementAction: JEV were made to adjust the Infrastructure Assets implemented by the Municipality that were transferred to the Barangay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate the MSWDO to formulate, for his approval, written policies or guidelines to provide stringent criteria on the grant of financial assistance as to the qualification of beneficiaries, the kind and amount of aid to be given, including control measures to avoid duplication, in order to preclude suspicion of favoritism, bias and irregularities in the process.     

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor minimize disbursements on financial assistance, and limit the same based on urgent and absolute necessity only, to ensure that the funds of the Municipality will not be easily depleted.
observations.managementAction:
An instruction was made to the MSWDO to formulate a written policies or guidelines on the grant of financial assistance.














They limit their financial assistance to P10,000.00 a day.
observations.reason:
However, because of the illness of the MSWDO, there was a hindrance to formulate the written policies or guidelines.













Partially Implemented

But, there were some rare cases that they exceeded the limitation specially for those constituents with urgent and absolute necessity.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the barangays who received funds from the Municipality to submit their respective Utilization Reports and refund the balance, if any. Also, deny additional request for assistance if the previous funds has not been fully accounted for.

We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer monitor whether the projects for which the funds were intended had been implemented, otherwise, require the immediate refund of the amount.
observations.managementAction:
All barangay with financial assistance have submitted the Utilization reports and have refunded the unutilized balance.  No additional assistance was made when the previous assistance is not liquidated.





The Municipal Engineer already monitored the implementation of the projects including the inspection upon the completion of the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate the MDRRMO to stop utilizing the LDRRMF for the payment of expenses which do not support disaster risk reduction management activities as specified under RA 10121 especially if such expenditures are not incorporated in the LDRRMIP approved by the Council to avoid audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: All expenses paid from LDRRMF were to support the disaster risk reduction management activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor terminate the services of consultants whose nature of work simply duplicate the functions of regular employees or whose work can be performed by existing regular personnel.  Henceforth, discontinue the hiring of persons with no professional expertise or special knowledge or training.
observations.managementAction: No consultant services were engaged that duplicates the functions of regular employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman, require the MCPC to prepare the MCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan to integrate the programs, projects and activities to be undertaken by the different departments of the LGU which can be attributed for MCPC.  Also, prioritized the PPAs according to needs for integration in the local development plan of the LGU and ensure that the funds for the PPAs is equivalent to one percent (1%) of the IRA of the current year.
observations.managementAction: The MCPC have prepared the Annual Work and Financial Plan for protection of children that integrate the programs, projects and activities to be undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board Council incorporate in the SEF budget programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development.
observations.managementAction: Programs and activities were incorporated in the SEF for Early Childhood Care Development.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System through its Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to give their full support and assistance to the GAD Focal Point to be able to: (a) submit the LGU’s GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG Office for review and endorsement; (b) implement fully all identified PPAs in the GAD Plan, and (c) prepare a detailed Accomplishment Report that will reflect the specific PPAs undertaken including the cost incurred.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide the GAD Focal Point with the necessary information on the actual expenditures incurred on GAD-related activities.
observations.managementAction:
A GAD plan and budget was submitted  to DILG for review and endorsement.  All identified PPAs in the GAD plan and budget were implemented.  GAD Accomplishment Report was also prepared at the end of the year. 







Actual expenditures on all GAD related activities were provided to the GAD Focal Point.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor conduct the quarterly meetings with the LEE Board and other officials concerned to monitor its operations and assess whether the activities of each LEE are geared towards the purpose for its creation.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the financial statements and the annual performance report of each LEE of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
Regular meeting was conducted to monitor and assess the operations of the LEE.





Separate financial statements for LEE was prepared by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following courses of actions:

The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.


The Inventory committee include in the conduct of the physical inventory of PPEs the local road networks and the preparation of Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network (RPCLRN);


The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant maintain the required Local Road Network Property and Ledger Cards, respectively.
observations.managementAction:
A description and cost segregation of the infrastructure projects was forwarded by the Municipal Engineer to the Municipal Accountant.




Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network was not prepared but they have an inventory of all infrastructure projects implemented during the year.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Engineer had the difficulty to determine the costs of the infrastructure projects from the prior year.

Partially Implemented

They have the difficulty to determine the cost of the prior year’s infrastructure projects because some of the projects were already relocked 


Not Implemented

No Local Road Network Property and Ledger Cards were prepared

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to record the accumulated depreciation of each public infrastructure account as of December 31, 2016 amounting to P2,645,498.48.  Also, compute monthly the accumulated depreciation of each public infrastructure in order to present the PPE at its depreciated replacement cost and record the appropriate expense account.
observations.managementAction: Only the depreciation of Power Supply System account was recorded.
observations.reason: No depreciation was made to other accounts of Infrastructure Asset accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned Department Heads accelerate the implementation of programs and projects funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA.  Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit immediately the Liquidation Reports and other supporting documents to the Source Agencies, including the return of unutilized fund of completed programs and projects.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2018, the funds transferred from the NGAs still amounted to P79 million, not all projects were implemented during the year.
observations.reason: Various reasons encountered by the Municipality for the complete implementation of the project.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC), through the Municipal Mayor as Chairman:

Include in the Annual Investment Plan only, the projects which can readily be implemented by the LGU during the year by considering the timetable in its implementation and requiring complete documentation prior to inclusion in the AIP.  

Provide appropriations on LGU equity only when required by the source agency, and;

Identify projects for the utilization of the balances of previous years’ 20% development fund to maximize its intended purpose. 

 Also, we recommend that the Municipal Engineer implement fully the projects contained in the AIP, through proper scheduling and prioritization of the projects to be implemented, to ensure optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction:
Projects included in the AIP were within the timetable to be implemented during the year.





Appropriations for LGU Equity were provided only when required by the source agency.

A supplemental budget was prepared for those balances from unimplemented projects from prior years.


Scheduling and prioritization on the implementation of the projects were undertaken.
observations.reason: Some projects were not yet implemented within the time schedule.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the management to immediately submit the required documents of the purchased real property or land in accordance with Section 13.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Article 35(c) of Administrative Order No. 270 to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: All documents on the purchase of lot were already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the management to submit the required certifications from the FDA for the procured drugs and medicines otherwise Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance shall be issued if not complied.  We further recommend for the Accountant to diligently check the required supporting documents in processing the transactions.
observations.managementAction: All procured drugs and medicines were dully supported with certificate from Food and Drug Administration.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend for management to strictly enforce the provisions on the grant, utilization and control of cash advances.  We reiterate the following courses of actions:

A demand shall be made by the Municipal Accountant for the immediate liquidation and settlement of all outstanding cash advances.  If the officials or employees concerned fail to liquidate after three successive reminders or letters, it shall be ground for withholding of their salaries corresponding to their unliquidated cash advance balances and the filing of criminal and administrative cases if evidences warrant.

Require the officials and employees concerned to immediately liquidate their cash advances for official travels within the prescribed period after the return to their permanent official station and as soon as the purpose for which it was given has been served.

Stop the practice of granting another cash advances to those officials and employees with outstanding cash advances.


Stop the practice of granting special cash advances for payment of regular procurement.  All purchases shall be paid thru checks except those paid under the petty cash fund.
observations.managementAction:
A demand letter was made to the officials and employees for the settlement of their unliquidated cash advances.






Travel cash advances of some employees were not liquidated within the prescribed period.






Some officials and employees were still granted another cash advances even they still have outstanding cash advance.

Most of the special cash advances granted were related to the rehabilitation from Typhoon Urduja.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should direct its Municipal Budget Officer to prepare budgetary allocations for PS with due regard on the mandated 45% PS Limitations to preclude excessive incurrence of expenses.
observations.managementAction: All budgetary allocation for PS were within the 45% PS limitation.

observations.recommendation:
Strictly adhere to proper management of inventory accounts thru the maintenance of complete records as well as compliance with such mandated activities as implementation of physical count.  Specifically the implementation of the following courses of actions is further proposed; viz:

Adhere to the policy of recording purchases and issuance of supplies inventory.


  Require the Property Custodian and the Accounting Department to maintain and update regularly stock card and supplies ledger cards for monitoring purposes;

Require the PC to conduct inventory taking periodically and submit inventory report on time to support the validity and reliability of the account balances of the Supplies Inventories
observations.managementAction:
Polices on the recording, purchases and issuances of supplies inventory were implemented.

Stock card and supplies ledger cards were maintained.



Periodic physical inventory was conducted for their supplies inventory.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry
Voucher to remove from the books of the LGU the Infrastructure Assets totaling
P4,491,064.00 transferred to the barangays based on the Certificate of Turn-Over
and Acceptance signed by the recipients Punong Barangays. Also, ensure that the
said properties are recorded by the Barangay Bookkeepers in the books of the
recipient barangays.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create the Disposal Committee
to initiate the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties pursuant to PD 1445
and require the said Committee to recommend the mode of its disposal. Also, to
require the Property Officer to follow the disposal procedures prescribed in RA No.
7160.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary
adjusting entries to derecognize from the books the properties after its proper
disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate the MSWDO to
formulate, for his approval, written policies or guidelines to provide stringent criteria
on the grant of financial assistance as to the qualification of beneficiaries, the kind
and amount of aid to be given, including control measures to avoid duplication, in
order to preclude suspicion of favoritism, bias and irregularities in the process.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor minimize disbursements on
financial assistance, and limit the same based on urgent and absolute necessity only,
to ensure that the funds of the Municipality will not be easily depleted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the barangays who
received funds from the Municipality to submit immediately their respective
Utilization Reports and refund the balance, if any. Also, stop the grant of additional
assistance to barangays whose previous financial assistance has not been fully
accounted for.
We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer monitor whether the
projects for which the funds were intended had been implemented, otherwise, require
the immediate refund of the amount.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer discontinue the payment of
regular expenses from her cash advances intended for specific purpose. Also, obtain
cash advance for financial assistance at an amount equivalent to the number of
identified recipients only and pay petty expenses from the petty cash fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate the MDRRMO to stop
utilizing the LDRRMF for the payment of expenses which do not support disaster
risk reduction management activities as specified under RA 10121. Also, ensure that
all expenditures are incorporated in the LDRRMIP approved by the Council to avoid
audit disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor terminate the services of
consultants whose nature of work simply duplicate the functions of regular employees
or whose work can be performed by existing regular personnel. Henceforth,
discontinue the hiring of persons with no professional expertise or special knowledge
or training.

We recommended that the MCPC prepare its Annual Work and Financial
Plan to integrate the programs, projects and activities to be undertaken by the
different departments of the LGU which can be attributed for MCPC. Also, prioritize
the PPAs according to needs for integration in the local development plan of the LGU
and ensure that the funds for the PPAs is equivalent to one percent (1%) of the IRA
of the current year.

We recommended that the Local School Board Council incorporate in the SEF
budget programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point
System through its Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to give their
full support and assistance to the GAD Focal Point to be able to: (a) submit the LGU’s
GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG Office for review and endorsement; (b)
implement fully all identified PPAs in the GAD Plan, and (c) prepare a detailed
Accomplishment Report that will reflect the specific PPAs undertaken including the
cost incurred.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide the GAD Focal
Point with the necessary information on the actual expenditures incurred on GAD-
related activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor conduct the quarterly meetings
with the LEE Board and other officials concerned to monitor its operations and assess
whether the activities of each LEE are geared towards the purpose for its creation.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and submit the financial statements and the annual
performance report of each LEE of the LGU.
