AAR CY 2010
observations.reason: The twelve (12) buses were not fully maintained; some units were preserved at dumpsite while others were under maintenance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following courses of actions:
(a) The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects.
(b) The Inventory committee include in the conduct of the physical inventory of
PPEs the local road networks and the preparation of Report on the Physical
Count of the Road Network (RPCLRN);
(c) The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant maintain the required Local Road
Network Property and Ledger Cards, respectively.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry
Voucher to record the accumulated depreciation of each public infrastructure
account as of December 31, 2016 amounting to P2,645,498.48. Also, compute
monthly the accumulated depreciation of each public infrastructure in order to
present the PPE at its depreciated replacement cost and record the appropriate
expense account.

We recommend that the concerned Department Heads accelerate the implementation
of programs and projects funded by the National Government in accordance with the
timeline provided for in the MOA. Also, the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer submit immediately the Liquidation Reports and other
supporting documents to the Source Agencies, including the return of unutilized fund
of completed programs and projects.

We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Council
(MPDC), through the Municipal Mayor as Chairman:
(a) include in the Annual Investment Plan only, the projects which can
readily be implemented by the LGU during the year by considering the
timetable in its implementation and requiring complete documentation
prior to inclusion in the AIP.
(b) provide appropriations on LGU equity only when required by the source
agency, and;
(c) identify projects for the utilization of the balances of previous years’ 20%
development fund to maximize its intended purpose.
Also, we recommend that the Municipal Engineer implement fully the
projects contained in the AIP, through proper scheduling and prioritization of the
projects to be implemented, to ensure optimal utilization of the 20% Development
Fund.

We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Council
(MPDC), through the Municipal Mayor, appropriate the 20% development fund
only for investments or capital expenditures and not for administrative programs.
Also, disbursements using the 20% development fund should be solely for
development projects.

We recommend that the Local School Board, through the Municipal Mayor
as Chairman, appropriate and utilize the Special Education Fund in accordance
with the purpose for which it was created particularly the
programs/projects/activities enumerated in DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular to
ensure that the purpose of the SEF are obtained and availed by the students.

We recommend that the Municipal Government require the Division Head of DepEd,
Biliran Division Office to submit a Fund Utilization Report to validate the actual
utilization of the funds. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant
record in the appropriate Due from NGA account all funds transferred to DepEd and
require the submission of complete and appropriate liquidation documents to prove
the validity and propriety of all disbursements using the SEF.

We recommend that the MDRRMO prepare the LDRRMFIP in the
prescribed format, which should also include the lump-sum appropriation for 30%
QRF and the separate caption for balances from the previous years in the Trust
Fund. Also, the MDRRMO, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget
Officer prepare the “Report on Sources and Utilization” on the LDRRMF every end
of the month and submit promptly to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of
each month.

We also recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer change the program
category for LDRRMF from “Budgetary Reserve” to “Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund” in the preparation of the Annual Budget of the Municipality.

We recommend that the members of the Solid Waste Management Board
fast-track the revision and submission of the MSWM Plan for approval by the
NSWMC to ensure compliance of the LGU of the requirements of the SWM Act.
Also, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor, through the SWM Focal
Person, enforce strictly the provisions of R.A. No. 9003, specifically on the proper
methods of solid waste collection, segregation and disposal as well as the prohibited
acts, to ensure safe and sanitary management of solid waste in the municipality.
